flow_vs_denoising.validity_delta: flow should maintain or improve validity

flow_vs_reranker.sampling_efficiency: flow should match or exceed reranker efficiency with fewer candidates

comparison_architecture.rationale: At matched_sampling_steps=50, flow_matching optimized with F2.1 loss weights (beta_intra_red=0.05, gamma_probe=0.15) competes against ablated denoising (standard weights) and reranker baseline. This tests whether flow claim (FC1: modular representation learning improves unseen-pocket generation) transfers to performance advantage.
flow_advantages: Optimized loss weights from F2.1 sweep
baseline_advantages: Denoising: Simple, proven noise schedule; longer diffusion steps
Reranker: Post-hoc selection from candidate pool; no trainable parameters for reranker component

result_summary.expected_outcome: Flow-matching demonstrates >=5% pocket fit improvement over denoising and >=8% over reranker, supporting FC1 (modular representation learning claim)

next_phase: Baseline results (F4.2 async execution complete); proceed to F5 reproducibility and narrative
